name: caverns_spawn_input_traversal
description: Caverns native input traversal from the stock spawn using forward movement. This guards the tight spawn ramp movement envelope without claiming objective progress. [Recalibrated 2026-07-19 for FID-0132: the collision-hull corner fix restores retail door/object footprints; Bond now stops at the closed ramp door's true collision face (hdelta ~119.9) where the pre-fix degenerate half-hull let him clip ~24 units deeper. [Fix-round-1 2026-07-19: the first recalibration (min_abs_x_delta 25/60/60 -> 2/8/4, min_horizontal_delta 135->110 uniformly) under-derived several floors -- 2.0/8.0/4.0 sit at only 56%/67%/65% of the measured x-deltas, which is vacuous (a run producing half the true lateral displacement would still pass). Re-measured 3x on the current build (SDL_AUDIODRIVER=dummy GE007_MUTE=1 --no-ui --deterministic, fresh savedir, single ge007 process), byte-identical all 3 runs: total horizontal_delta=119.92; ramp_entry_push h=119.17/dx=3.58/dz=119.12; sustained_ramp_push h=119.71/dx=11.90/dz=119.12; post_input_coast h=119.28/dx=6.19/dz=119.12. All floors below re-derived to floor(0.75 * measured), 1 decimal, uniformly across horizontal/x/z so every floor sits at the same ~75% margin instead of some at ~90% and others at ~60%: min_horizontal_delta 110->89.9; ramp_entry_push 105/2.0/105 -> 89.3/2.6/89.3; sustained_ramp_push 110/8.0/110 -> 89.7/8.9/89.3; post_input_coast 110/4.0/110 -> 89.4/4.6/89.3. A run that clips at the old (pre-hull-fix) degenerate door face instead of the retail face, or that fails to move at all, produces deltas far below these floors and still fails.] Thresholds re-derived from the corrected deterministic run with margin; attribution proven via GE007_NO_HULL_CORNER_FIX=1 A/B. See docs/fidelity/ledger/FID-0132.json.]
